title: Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 31, 2026
summary: Sudan's civil war reached Day 1081 with the RSF-SPLM-N siege of Dilling (South Kordofan) entering its third day, Habila road severed and basic commodities depleted; the Sudan Doctors Network warned of an El Fasher-scale catastrophe. In Blue Nile, RSF-SPLM-N shifted focus south toward Geissan threatening the Roseires Dam and Ed Damazin, with 73,000+ civilians displaced into the state capital since Eid. MSF published a landmark report documenting 3,396 sexual violence survivors (97% women and girls) treated at MSF Darfur facilities between January 2024 and November 2025, calling RSF use of rape a systematic 'weapon of war.' The UN condemned escalating drone attacks on civilians in Sudan as potentially constituting war crimes, and the MSF sexual violence figure updated the tracker's casualty data.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: Sudan's civil war reached Day 1079 with a major RSF-SPLM-N ground assault on Dilling, South Kordofan, killing at least 14 civilians (including 5 children and 2 women) and wounding 23 over March 28-29; the Sudan Doctors Network warned of an El Fasher-scale catastrophe as the road to Habila was severed. In Blue Nile, 425+ families fled Geissan amid RSF-SPLM-N military buildup threatening Ed Damazin and the Roseires Dam. SAF drone strikes continued on RSF-held Zalingei (IDP camp area hit) and Al Sunut (7 killed at funeral, 39 wounded). The US pushed a UN Security Council humanitarian ceasefire resolution while the UNSC imposed targeted sanctions on four RSF commanders, including Hemedti's brother Abdul Rahim Dagalo, for El Fasher atrocities.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 28, 2026
summary: Sudan's civil war entered Day 1078 with the RSF-SPLM-N alliance capturing Kurmuk in Blue Nile State on March 23, opening a new front that threatens Ed Damazin, the state capital, and the Roseires Dam. SAF drones struck Lagawa (West Kordofan) twice — March 23 and 27 — killing 30+ in residential areas, while an RSF drone struck Ad Dabbah in Northern State on March 21 killing 6 and damaging power infrastructure. RSF forces massacred 16 civilians in El Fasher on March 25, continuing post-capture atrocities in occupied North Darfur. New UN envoy Pekka Haavisto met SAF chief Burhan in Port Sudan on March 26 without reaching any ceasefire agreement. The total killed in healthcare attacks since April 2023 surpassed 2,000 following the Eid al-Fitr Al Daein hospital strike.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: Sudan's civil war escalated dramatically in mid-to-late March 2026 with a devastating surge in drone warfare killing over 500 civilians since January 1, and more than 200 in just the two weeks following March 4. On Eid al-Fitr (March 20), air and drone strikes struck El Daein Teaching Hospital in East Darfur, killing at least 64 people including 13 children — pushing the total killed in healthcare attacks above 2,000 since the war began. On the battlefield, the RSF recaptured the strategic city of Bara in North Kordofan on March 16 and simultaneously seized the border town of Karnoi, continuing its strategy to encircle SAF headquarters at El Obeid. The conflict spilled further into Chad when an RSF drone killed 17 people in the border town of Tine on March 18, prompting President Déby to close Sudan's 1,300 km border and threaten military retaliation.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 22, 2026
summary: Sudan's catastrophic civil war enters day 1072 with two formally declared famines in El-Fasher and Kadugli, and 400,000 people facing starvation. OHCHR published findings in February 2026 concluding RSF violations during the capture of El Fasher constitute war crimes. Fighting intensified in Kordofan in early 2026 with almost daily drone strikes on civilian infrastructure including markets and health facilities. The UN Fact-Finding Mission previously concluded the RSF committed genocide against non-Arab Indigenous groups. Sudan hosts the world's largest humanitarian crisis with 24.6 million food insecure and 12.1 million displaced.
